title: Code of Conduct
text: project follows Contributor Covenant 2.1. expect open, welcoming, harassment-free environment. Abusive discriminatory behavior, behavior exposes personal data, tolerated. Incidents can reported confidentially maintainer leite@castlab.org. reports reviewed fairly respectfully.

title: Contributing to obrasgov
text: Thank interest contributing.

title: Contributing to obrasgov
text: bugs behavior changes, open issue describing case include minimal reproducible example. Never include personal data, credentials, non-public information. Interface changes must use English snake_case names follow tidyverse style guide. Preserve Portuguese names values come directly upstream ObrasGov API contract.

title: Contributing to obrasgov
text: Create fork branch change. Install dependencies pak::pak(). Add update tests tests/testthat/. Run devtools::document(), devtools::test(), devtools::check(). Update NEWS.md change affects users. Integration tests official API optional can enabled : participating, agree follow Code Conduct.
code: OBRASGOV_LIVE_TESTS=true R -q -e 'devtools::test()'

title: An end-to-end ObrasGov workflow
text: Keep filter values retrieval limits object. makes scope easy review store result. sending request, confirm chosen filters exist:
code: project_query <- list(   uf_principal = "PE",   situacao = "Em execução",   page_size = 200,   all_pages = TRUE,   page_limit = 5 )  project_query #> $uf_principal #> [1] "PE" #>  #> $situacao #> [1] "Em execução" #>  #> $page_size #> [1] 200 #>  #> $all_pages #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $page_limit #> [1] 5 available_project_filters <- list_filters("projects")$filter requested_filters <- c("uf_principal", "situacao")  all(requested_filters %in% available_project_filters) #> [1] TRUE

title: An end-to-end ObrasGov workflow
text: Use rlang::exec() pass named list arguments get_projects(). Always inspect metadata limited multi-page request. pages_retrieved equals page_limit total_pages larger, result intentionally incomplete.
code: projects <- rlang::exec(get_projects, !!!project_query)  projects |>   dplyr::select(     id_projeto_investimento,     desc_nome,     situacao,     dt_inicial_prevista,     dt_final_prevista   ) project_metadata <- result_metadata(projects)  project_metadata[c(   "total_items",   "total_pages",   "pages_retrieved",   "retrieved_at" )]

title: An end-to-end ObrasGov workflow
text: Related endpoint queries limited projects needed analysis. example uses first ten unique identifiers.
code: project_ids <- projects |>   dplyr::distinct(id_projeto_investimento) |>   dplyr::slice_head(n = 10) |>   dplyr::pull(id_projeto_investimento)  project_ids

title: An end-to-end ObrasGov workflow
text: Map identifiers combine returned tibbles. request narrow relationship project table explicit. pattern retrieves contracts: financial analysis, replace get_contracts() get_commitments(). Feasibility studies project status histories follow pattern.
code: physical_execution <- project_ids |>   purrr::map(function(project_id) {     get_physical_execution(       id_projeto_investimento = project_id,       all_pages = TRUE,       page_limit = 5     )   }) |>   purrr::list_rbind() contracts <- project_ids |>   purrr::map(function(project_id) {     get_contracts(       id_projeto_investimento = project_id,       all_pages = TRUE,       page_limit = 5     )   }) |>   purrr::list_rbind()

what: 5. Join project attributes to related records
title: An end-to-end ObrasGov workflow
text: following local example demonstrates join without contacting API. column names mirror ObrasGov response fields used . Join execution data one row per project: Contracts form one--many relationship. Summarize joining desired result one row per project:
code: projects_example <- tibble::tibble(   id_projeto_investimento = c("100.00-01", "200.00-02"),   desc_nome = c("School renovation", "Health unit construction"),   situacao = c("Em execução", "Em execução") )  execution_example <- tibble::tibble(   id_projeto_investimento = c("100.00-01", "200.00-02"),   percentual_execucao_fisica = c(72.5, 35.0),   dt_atualizacao_execucao = as.Date(c("2026-06-30", "2026-07-10")) )  contracts_example <- tibble::tibble(   id_projeto_investimento = c("100.00-01", "100.00-01", "200.00-02"),   id_contrato = c(11L, 12L, 21L),   valor_global_contrato = c(1200000, 300000, 2450000) ) project_execution <- projects_example |>   dplyr::left_join(     execution_example,     by = "id_projeto_investimento"   )  project_execution #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   id_projeto_investimento desc_nome              situacao percentual_execucao_…¹ #>   <chr>                   <chr>                  <chr>                     <dbl> #> 1 100.00-01               School renovation      Em exec…                   72.5 #> 2 200.00-02               Health unit construct… Em exec…                   35   #> # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​percentual_execucao_fisica #> # ℹ 1 more variable: dt_atualizacao_execucao <date> contract_summary <- contracts_example |>   dplyr::group_by(id_projeto_investimento) |>   dplyr::summarise(     contract_count = dplyr::n(),     total_contract_value = sum(valor_global_contrato, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  analysis_table <- project_execution |>   dplyr::left_join(     contract_summary,     by = "id_projeto_investimento"   )  analysis_table #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   id_projeto_investimento desc_nome              situacao percentual_execucao_…¹ #>   <chr>                   <chr>                  <chr>                     <dbl> #> 1 100.00-01               School renovation      Em exec…                   72.5 #> 2 200.00-02               Health unit construct… Em exec…                   35   #> # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​percentual_execucao_fisica #> # ℹ 3 more variables: dt_atualizacao_execucao <date>, contract_count <int>, #> #   total_contract_value <dbl>

title: An end-to-end ObrasGov workflow
text: Save data together original query, pagination metadata, package version, retrieval time, source update timestamp. saved object now contains analytical inputs enough context audit repeat retrieval later.
code: provenance <- list(   query = project_query,   project_metadata = result_metadata(projects),   source_updated_at = get_last_update(),   package_version = utils::packageVersion("obrasgov"),   saved_at = Sys.time() )  saveRDS(   list(     projects = projects,     physical_execution = physical_execution,     contracts = contracts,     provenance = provenance   ),   "obrasgov-pe-workflow.rds" )

title: Introduction to obrasgov
text: Start list_resources(). function entirely local contact API. function_name column identifies function used query resource. main package functions use English names. API’s filter names, response fields, categorical values remain Portuguese part official ObrasGov contract.
code: list_resources() #> # A tibble: 8 × 4 #>   resource            function_name           endpoint                 paginated #>   <chr>               <chr>                   <chr>                    <lgl>     #> 1 projects            get_projects            projeto-investimento     TRUE      #> 2 commitments         get_commitments         empenho                  TRUE      #> 3 physical_execution  get_physical_execution  execucao-fisica          TRUE      #> 4 contracts           get_contracts           contrato                 TRUE      #> 5 geometries          get_geometries          geometria                TRUE      #> 6 status_history      get_status_history      historico-situacao-canc… TRUE      #> 7 feasibility_studies get_feasibility_studies estudo-viabilidade       TRUE      #> 8 last_update         get_last_update         data-atualizacao         FALSE

title: Introduction to obrasgov
text: Use list_filters() see accepted filter names expected types. example, projects can filtered state, status, registration year, dates, several attributes. Allowed categorical values stored allowed_values list-column. following code retrieves values accepted situacao filter: Dates can supplied Date values. package serializes using ISO YYYY-MM-DD format expected API.
code: project_filters <- list_filters("projects") project_filters[c("filter", "type")] #> # A tibble: 29 × 2 #>    filter                  type      #>    <chr>                   <chr>     #>  1 id_projeto_investimento character #>  2 projeto_estruturante    character #>  3 desc_nome               character #>  4 nr_cep                  character #>  5 desc_endereco           character #>  6 desc_projeto            character #>  7 desc_funcao_social      character #>  8 desc_meta_global        character #>  9 dt_inicial_prevista     Date      #> 10 dt_final_prevista       Date      #> # ℹ 19 more rows project_filters$allowed_values[   project_filters$filter == "situacao" ][[1]] #> [1] "Cadastrada"  "Cancelada"   "Concluída"   "Em execução" "Inacabada"   #> [6] "Paralisada"

title: Introduction to obrasgov
text: Begin narrow query small page_size. Network calls run vignette built, installation CRAN checks depend external service. returned object tibble. Date-like fields dt_cadastro converted Date every non-missing value uses ISO date format. Nested one--many relationships preserved list-columns.
code: projects_pe <- get_projects(   uf_principal = "PE",   situacao = "Em execução",   dt_cadastro = as.Date("2024-01-01"),   page_size = 25 )  projects_pe

title: Introduction to obrasgov
text: Every paginated result carries retrieval metadata. Check deciding whether pages required. initial request retrieves one page. collect pages safely, use all_pages = TRUE together finite page_limit. See vignette("pagination--nested-data") details.
code: metadata <- result_metadata(projects_pe)  metadata$total_items metadata$total_pages metadata$pages_retrieved metadata$retrieved_at

title: Introduction to obrasgov
text: ObrasGov resources can related id_projeto_investimento. selecting project, use identifier retrieve physical execution, contracts, commitments, status history, feasibility studies. Filtering related endpoints project identifier avoids downloading large tables makes relationship explicit analysis code.
code: project_id <- projects_pe$id_projeto_investimento[[1]]  physical_execution <- get_physical_execution(   id_projeto_investimento = project_id )  contracts <- get_contracts(   id_projeto_investimento = project_id )  commitments <- get_commitments(   id_projeto_investimento = project_id )  status_history <- get_status_history(   id_projeto_investimento = project_id )

title: Introduction to obrasgov
text: API reports time recent data load. Store value query results identify temporal version source.
code: source_updated_at <- get_last_update() source_updated_at

title: Introduction to obrasgov
text: original Portuguese function names remain available compatibility aliases. Paginated aliases retain original pagination argument names. New code use get_projects(), page_size, all_pages.
code: projetos_pe <- obter_projetos(   uf_principal = "PE",   tamanho_da_pagina = 25,   todas_paginas = FALSE )

title: Introduction to obrasgov
text: API require authentication. Three options customize transport without changing every function call: default, client requests HTTP/2 TLS, retries transient failures, limits access 60 requests per minute.
code: options(   obrasgov.base_url = "https://api-publica.obrasgov.gestao.gov.br/obras",   obrasgov.timeout = 30,   obrasgov.user_agent = "my-project/1.0 (contact@example.org)" )

title: Introduction to obrasgov
text: Read vignette("pagination--nested-data") collect multiple pages normalize list-columns. Read vignette("end--end-workflow") build reproducible dataset multiple ObrasGov resources.

title: Pagination and nested data
text: Paginated functions retrieve one page default. prevents overly broad query triggering thousands requests unintentionally. page selects first page, page_size controls many records API can return per page. supported page size 1 200.
code: projects <- get_projects(   uf_principal = "PE",   page = 1,   page_size = 200 )  result_metadata(projects)

what: Collect a controlled number of pages
title: Pagination and nested data
text: Set all_pages = TRUE combine successive pages one tibble. exploration, also set finite page_limit maximum number requests visible code. Collection starts page. Therefore, page = 4 page_limit = 3 retrieve pages 4, 5, 6. Use page_limit = Inf complete result required. Requests performed sequentially, package’s retry throttling policies remain active.
code: projects_sample <- get_projects(   uf_principal = "PE",   all_pages = TRUE,   page_limit = 3,   page_size = 200 )  result_metadata(projects_sample)$pages_retrieved projects_window <- get_projects(   uf_principal = "PE",   page = 4,   all_pages = TRUE,   page_limit = 3,   page_size = 200 )

title: Pagination and nested data
text: project may multiple executors, recipients, transferors, policy areas, funding sources, geometries. relationships preserved list-columns instead reduced first item. following API-shaped example created locally transformations executed vignette built: Inspect one cell without changing project table:
code: projects <- tibble::tibble(   id_projeto_investimento = c("100.00-01", "200.00-02"),   desc_nome = c("School renovation", "Health unit construction"),   executores = list(     list(       list(nome_organizacao = "Municipality A", cnpj = "00000000000100"),       list(nome_organizacao = "State Agency", cnpj = "00000000000200")     ),     list(       list(nome_organizacao = "Municipality B", cnpj = "00000000000300")     )   ) )  projects #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   id_projeto_investimento desc_nome                executores #>   <chr>                   <chr>                    <list>     #> 1 100.00-01               School renovation        <list [2]> #> 2 200.00-02               Health unit construction <list [1]> projects$executores[[1]] #> [[1]] #> [[1]]$nome_organizacao #> [1] "Municipality A" #>  #> [[1]]$cnpj #> [1] "00000000000100" #>  #>  #> [[2]] #> [[2]]$nome_organizacao #> [1] "State Agency" #>  #> [[2]]$cnpj #> [1] "00000000000200"

title: Pagination and nested data
text: Use tidyr::unnest_longer() create one row per executor tidyr::unnest_wider() turn executor fields columns. Keeping normalization explicit avoids multiplying unrelated nested relationships. executors funding sources unnested together, every executor paired every funding source project.
code: executors <- projects |>   dplyr::select(id_projeto_investimento, executores) |>   tidyr::unnest_longer(executores) |>   tidyr::unnest_wider(executores)  executors #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   id_projeto_investimento nome_organizacao cnpj           #>   <chr>                   <chr>            <chr>          #> 1 100.00-01               Municipality A   00000000000100 #> 2 100.00-01               State Agency     00000000000200 #> 3 200.00-02               Municipality B   00000000000300

title: Pagination and nested data
text: Pages combined tibble returned, ordinary tidyverse code need know page contained record.
code: projects_sample |>   dplyr::count(situacao, sort = TRUE)

title: Pagination and nested data
text: reproducible work, store query, pagination metadata, API update timestamp data. bundle records requested, much retrieved, request ran, ObrasGov last updated source data.
code: query <- list(   uf_principal = "PE",   all_pages = TRUE,   page_limit = 3,   page_size = 200 )  provenance <- list(   query = query,   pagination = result_metadata(projects_sample),   source_updated_at = get_last_update() )  saveRDS(   list(data = projects_sample, provenance = provenance),   "projects-pe.rds" )

what: obrasgov | Brazilian public infrastructure data in R
title: obrasgov | Brazilian public infrastructure data in R
text: obrasgov provides modern R interface ObrasGov Open Data API, maintained Brazilian federal government. retrieves data infrastructure projects, physical execution, budget commitments, contracts, geometries, feasibility studies, status histories suspended cancelled projects.

title: obrasgov | Brazilian public infrastructure data in R
text: official API extensive paginated. Using directly requires building URLs, handling HTTP errors, joining pages, transforming nested JSON. obrasgov handles tasks returns analysis-ready tibbles without discarding one--many relationships. designed researchers, oversight bodies, public managers, data journalists, civil society organizations. Key features include: consistent English snake_case interface, Portuguese compatibility aliases; HTTP/2 TLS negotiation, retries, limit 60 requests per minute; explicit pagination retrieval metadata; dates converted Date update timestamp converted POSIXct; nested objects preserved list-columns; offline unit tests optional integration tests official API. API’s filter names, response fields, categorical values remain Portuguese part upstream ObrasGov contract.

title: obrasgov | Brazilian public infrastructure data in R
text: package development yet CRAN. Install GitHub version :
code: install.packages("pak") pak::pak("StrategicProjects/obrasgov")

title: obrasgov | Brazilian public infrastructure data in R
text: Inspect resource filter names without accessing internet: Retrieving multiple pages must requested explicitly: Portuguese aliases remain available compatibility. retain original Portuguese pagination argument names: See vignette("obrasgov") complete introduction, vignette("end--end-workflow") reproducible multi-resource analysis, vignette("pagination--nested-data") pagination list-columns.
code: library(obrasgov)  projects_pe <- get_projects(   uf_principal = "PE",   situacao = "Em execução",   page_size = 100 )  projects_pe result_metadata(projects_pe) list_resources() list_filters("projects") contracts <- get_contracts(   id_projeto_investimento = "134851.26-07",   all_pages = TRUE,   page_limit = 20 ) projetos_pe <- obter_projetos(   uf_principal = "PE",   tamanho_da_pagina = 100 )

title: obrasgov | Brazilian public infrastructure data in R
text: package exclusively uses current environment https://api-publica.obrasgov.gestao.gov.br/obras. previous API scheduled discontinued August 31, 2026, supported.

title: Retrieve budget commitments — get_commitments
text: Retrieve budget commitments

title: Retrieve budget commitments — get_commitments
code: get_commitments(   ...,   page = 1L,   page_size = 50L,   all_pages = FALSE,   page_limit = Inf,   base_url = .obrasgov_base_url() )  obter_empenhos(   ...,   pagina = 1L,   tamanho_da_pagina = 50L,   todas_paginas = FALSE,   limite_paginas = Inf,   base_url = .obrasgov_base_url() )

title: Retrieve budget commitments — get_commitments
text: ... Named filters. See list_filters("commitments"). page First page retrieve, starting 1. page_size Number records per page, 1 200. all_pages TRUE, retrieves successive pages starting page. page_limit Maximum number pages retrieve all_pages TRUE. Use Inf retrieve every available page. base_url HTTPS base URL. default, uses obrasgov.base_url option official API environment. pagina, tamanho_da_pagina, todas_paginas, limite_paginas Portuguese aliases page, page_size, all_pages, page_limit, respectively. arguments available Portuguese function alias.

title: Retrieve project status histories — get_status_history

See list_filters("status_history"). page First page retrieve, starting 1. page_size Number records per page, 1 200. all_pages TRUE, retrieves successive pages starting page. page_limit Maximum number pages retrieve all_pages TRUE. Use Inf retrieve every available page. base_url HTTPS base URL. default, uses obrasgov.base_url option official API environment. pagina, tamanho_da_pagina, todas_paginas, limite_paginas Portuguese aliases page, page_size, all_pages, page_limit, respectively. arguments available Portuguese function alias.

what: obrasgov: Access ObrasGov open data — obrasgov-package
title: obrasgov: Access ObrasGov open data — obrasgov-package
text: obrasgov provides modern, typed interface ObrasGov open data API. API resource represented function returns tibble::tibble(), preserving nested relationships list-columns.

title: obrasgov: Access ObrasGov open data — obrasgov-package
text: Requests use HTTP/2 TLS supported libcurl, automatic retries transient failures responsible request throttling. API require authentication.

title: obrasgov: Access ObrasGov open data — obrasgov-package
text: obrasgov.base_url: alternative API base URL. obrasgov.timeout: timeout request, seconds. obrasgov.user_agent: alternative HTTP user agent.

title: obrasgov 0.1.0
text: Rebuilt package new ObrasGov open data API. Added consistent snake_case functions eight public resources. Added HTTP/2 TLS negotiation, transient retries, request throttling actionable API errors. Added safe multi-page collection, typed date columns, pagination metadata lossless nested relationships. Added offline unit tests, optional live integration tests, vignettes, pkgdown configuration continuous integration workflows. Made English names canonical across public interface documentation, retaining Portuguese compatibility aliases. Added step--step vignettes resource discovery, safe pagination, nested data normalization, multi-resource joins, reproducible provenance.
